title: hooks: harden pre-commit-checks.sh — worktree-push guard + Bead-trailer block
description:
## Context
Two git-discipline rules are text-only today. (1) /commit's "worktree
exception" — subagents don't push; the orchestrator merges + pushes —
sits as a paragraph below a `git push` heading, easy to skim past; a
worktree push leaves stale worktree-agent-* remote branches. (2)
pre-commit-checks.sh only WARNS on a missing `Bead:` trailer (exit 0) —
an invisible warning under token pressure; the gap surfaces later as
`br orphans`.

## Task
Harden pre-commit-checks.sh (PreToolUse:Bash):
1. Block `git push` when cwd is inside .claude/worktrees/agent-*.
2. Upgrade the missing-Bead-trailer check from warn to block, exempting
   meta-commits (:card_file_box: / :broom: / :dollar: gitmoji — the
   bead-state / triage / offboard / cost commits that reference beads
   in the subject, not a trailer).
Preserve the git-add-A block, bead-sync, and staged-lint behaviour.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] git push from a worktree cwd blocked; from project root, allowed
- [ ] commit with no Bead: trailer blocked
- [ ] :card_file_box: / :broom: / :dollar: meta-commits exempt
- [ ] git-add-A block + bead-sync + staged-lint preserved
- [ ] new test-pre-commit-checks.sh green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] worktree-push guard blocks git push from a worktree cwd
 - [ ] missing Bead: trailer blocks; meta-commits (:card_file_box:/:broom:/:dollar:) exempt
 - [ ] git-add-A block + staged-lint preserved; new test green

title: skills: genericize handoff-templates + housekeeping attributions
description:
## Context
handoff-templates.md and housekeeping/SKILL.md are distributed reference
files (they ship to the team via /distribute). They carried a personal
attribution ("Luca Ronin") and LinearB-fleet specifics (Asana / "GTM
Engineering project"), plus a now-dangling /asana cross-reference.
Generic harness infrastructure should not name specific people or
internal projects. Surfaced 2026-05-21 during the verbosity-pass review.

## Task
Strip the personal ADR attribution from both files; genericize the
receipt-of-work pattern in handoff-templates.md (drop Asana/GTM
specifics and the dead /asana cross-ref; keep the generic "a receipt is
not a bead type" lesson); make the eval path a <project> placeholder.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No personal-name or LinearB-internal references remain
- [ ] No instructional content lost — only attribution/specificity removed
- [ ] Adversarial review (/scrutinize) verdict: SHIP
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] handoff-templates.md + housekeeping genericized
 - [ ] no instructional content lost
 - [ ] /scrutinize verdict SHIP

title: gitignore: ignore .bv/ beads-viewer local config
description:
## Context
beads-viewer (bv) drops local config + caches under .bv/ in any beads-using repo. Those shouldn't be tracked.

## Task
Add .bv/ to .gitignore.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] .bv/ in .gitignore
- [x] No bv local cache tracked

title: hooks: add post-gen-fanout-check.sh — gamma/openrouter fan-out reminder
description:
## Context
/gamma and /openrouter are cost-aware skills — one generation per call,
confirm cost first, never fan out variants on speculation. That
discipline is text-only. The "never fire autonomously" half depends on
user intent (unobservable to a hook), but the speculative-loop pattern
— several generations in quick succession — IS mechanically visible.

## Task
Add post-gen-fanout-check.sh, a PostToolUse:Bash hook. It counts
successful gamma-render.sh / openrouter-image.sh invocations per session
in a rolling 10-minute window and prints a one-line stderr reminder on
the 2nd+. WARN only — never blocks (the call already ran; the user may
legitimately want it). Wire it into settings.json.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] post-gen-fanout-check.sh created; PostToolUse:Bash, exits 0 always
- [ ] 2nd+ generation in the window prints a reminder; the 1st does not
- [ ] failed generations (exit_code != 0) not counted
- [ ] wired into settings.json PostToolUse Bash array
- [ ] test-post-gen-fanout-check.sh green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] post-gen-fanout-check.sh: PostToolUse:Bash, WARN-only, exits 0 always
 - [ ] 2nd+ generation in window warns; failed generations not counted
 - [ ] wired into settings.json; test green

title: onboard: promote skill-loading to its own gated step
description:
## Context
When a user invokes /onboard, the agent unreliably reads all skill
bodies — it skips some, or delegates the read to an Explore agent whose
context is then discarded. The user has had to explicitly say "read all
skills directly, no Explore agent" every session. The skill already
said to do this, but as buried item 4 of Step 1, framed as an outcome
rather than a forcing procedure.

## Task
Promote skill-loading to its own numbered step (Step 2): an
enumerate-via-ls read list, an explicit forbidden-shortcuts list
(Explore-agent delegation / sampling / skip-because-long), and a
verification gate — the orientation report must state "Skills loaded:
N/N". Renumber subsequent steps and fix cross-references.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill-loading is its own step with a concrete ls procedure
- [ ] Forbidden shortcuts named explicitly
- [ ] Orientation report gates on the N/N count
- [ ] Step renumbering + cross-refs consistent
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] skill-loading is its own step with an ls procedure
 - [ ] forbidden shortcuts named; report gates on N/N count
 - [ ] step renumbering + cross-refs consistent

title: skills: genericize fix/SKILL.md — dangling /asana ref + LinearB receipt-of-work specifics
description:
## Context
Flagged by Agent A (/scrutinize pass on the 2026-05-21 verbosity work):
fix/SKILL.md still references a `/asana` SKILL "Receipt-of-work ticket"
section that no longer exists (that content moved to
~/linearb/refs/asana-fleet.md in the prior asana-skill genericization).
fix/SKILL.md also carries LinearB-fleet specifics inline — "GTM
Engineering project / board", "Asana the same day", "Notify in Slack",
"fleet integration is available" — the same class as the
handoff-templates / housekeeping genericization, just missed at the
time.

## Task
Genericize fix/SKILL.md to match handoff-templates / housekeeping /
asana / gdoc:
1. Replace "Step 4: Asana log (LinearB / fleet agents only)" with a
   team-agnostic "external receipt log" section, with a small LinearB
   pointer at ~/linearb/refs/asana-* for the fleet-specific mechanics.
2. Fix the dangling `/asana SKILL "Receipt-of-work ticket" section`
   cross-ref (the asana skill no longer has that section).
3. Genericize the production-hotfix-path bullets that name Asana / Slack.
4. Genericize the anti-pattern that calls out "asana log" / "fleet
   integration."

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No dangling /asana cross-reference
- [ ] No Asana / Slack / GTM Engineering / "fleet integration" specifics
      inline; LinearB context behind a pointer to ~/linearb/refs/
- [ ] Generic lesson preserved (file external receipts for material fixes;
      bead is enough for routine)
- [ ] No instructional content lost
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] dangling /asana cross-reference removed
 - [ ] LinearB-specifics moved behind a ~/linearb/refs/ pointer
 - [ ] generic receipt-of-work lesson preserved

title: skills: verbosity trim pass per the 2026-05-21 audit
description:
## Context
The 2026-05-21 4-agent skill audit flagged most technical how-to skills
as 15-35% trimmable. The biggest wins were already absorbed by the
2026-05-21 restructure: /asana rewritten lean (~85 lines), /openrouter's
54-line personal section cut, /gdoc's connector-dump extracted, /duckdb
retired entirely. This bead tracks the RESIDUAL trims — pure polish, no
defects; the skills are committed and functional.

## Task — trim per the audit's specific findings
- gamma (~25-30%): the 3 Style A/B/C JSONC payloads duplicate
  reference/3-styles.md + reference/example-deterministic.json — keep
  one inline, reference the rest; "Common workflows" and "Guardrails"
  restate the "CRITICAL: cost awareness" section — consolidate.
- beads (~20%): the bv section duplicates reference/bv.md (command
  table + jq recipes); the ADR-conventions block duplicates
  reference/handoff-templates.md. KEEP "Choosing a type" + "Stages vs
  gates" in the body — the user explicitly wants them there.
- nginx (~10%): the certbot-managed-vhost gotcha is stated twice in
  full — collapse the second to a back-reference.
- lint (~5-10%, minor): "Real-world recovery patterns" overlaps the
  "When to disable a rule" decision tree.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each skill trimmed per the findings; no loss of load-bearing content
- [ ] /distribute run afterward so the team copy stays current

title: hooks: harden pre-bead-close.sh — worktree guard + scrutiny gate
description:
## Context
"Subagents never run br close / br update --status; bead lifecycle is
orchestrator-only" is repeated across AGENTS.md, /beads, /orchestrator,
/handoff — repeated that often because it gets broken. A subagent
finishing work has the bead ID and br on PATH; the .beads/ symlink
makes a stray close mutate the orchestrator's shared state immediately.
Separately, /scrutinize says an -t impl bead closes only on a SHIP
verdict, but nothing enforces it.

## Task
Add two guards to pre-bead-close.sh (PreToolUse:Bash):
1. Worktree guard — when cwd is inside .claude/worktrees/agent-*, block
   `br close` and `br update --status` (still allow `br update --notes`,
   which /beads explicitly permits subagents to do).
2. Scrutiny gate — closing a `-t impl` bead is blocked unless its
   br-show output carries a SHIP (or OVERRIDE) scrutiny verdict.
Preserve the existing lint gate and chained-cd handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worktree cwd blocks br close + br update --status; allows br update --notes
- [ ] Non-worktree cwd unaffected (orchestrator runs lifecycle normally)
- [ ] -t impl bead with no SHIP/OVERRIDE verdict blocked; with one, allowed
- [ ] Existing lint gate + chained-cd behaviour preserved
- [ ] test-pre-bead-close.sh extended and green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] worktree guard: blocks br close + br update --status, allows br update --notes
 - [ ] scrutiny gate: -t impl beads need a SHIP/OVERRIDE verdict to close
 - [ ] existing lint + chained-cd behaviour preserved; test green

title: zig-zone: private mesh + workshop/server split via Tailscale
description:
## 1. Overview

`zig-zone` is the user's private mesh — a Tailscale tailnet wrapping 5 devices into one virtual LAN regardless of physical network:

- `zig-computer` — Ubuntu VPS, public IP `51.81.33.136`, OVH KVM
- `pico` — Mac Studio M1 Max 64GB, headless, on home LAN
- `metis` — personal MacBook
- `zig` — work MacBook (subject to OQ-03 — work device policy)
- iPhone (Tailscale iOS app + Termius SSH client)

**The split this spec enables.** zig-computer becomes the **workshop + edge**: it stays the user's primary coding box (Claude Code, dotfiles, agent harness) AND retains the public surface (nginx for `*.zig.computer`, reef-router on :7575 for the granola webhook, sshd on :22 as break-glass). pico becomes the **server farm**: it runs Ollama (LLM inference), `vs14-web`, the SS14 game server, the vacation-station postgres dbs, and the Docker observability stack. nginx on zig-computer reverse-proxies inbound HTTP/UDP/TCP through the tailnet to pico. All inter-machine SSH happens primarily via Tailscale SSH.

**Why.** The user wants the workshop machine free from interruption — services on the same box as your active development creates risk of accidental kills, port conflicts, resource pressure mid-flow. The Mac Studio is purpose-built for sustained server load (silent, efficient, Apple Silicon GPU for inference). Plus: the M1 Max's 64GB unified memory gives a real local-LLM serving option that the OVH x86 VM can't match.

**In scope.** Mesh bootstrap, ACL, the 5-phase service migration from zig-computer → pico, the dotfiles changes to install + configure Tailscale + Ollama on both OSes, the zig-zone skill/runbook.

**Out of scope.** LoRA fine-tuning workflow (deferred to a future spec — MLX-LM + Python venv on pico). Headscale migration (future). Subnet routing (no other home-LAN devices need exposure). Additional VPS/devices. Tailscale Funnel (we have a VPS w/ public IP already).

**Sources informing this spec.**
- Three Explore-agent research briefs run 2026-05-23 covering: Tailscale fundamentals + ACLs + SSH, VPS-as-edge w/ nginx stream{} forwarding, Mac Studio LLM stack (Ollama/MLX/LM Studio)
- `~/explore/.claude/skills/zig-computer-audit/SKILL.md` — current machine topology, services, firewall posture
- Live audit run 2026-05-23: 34 active services on zig-computer, UFW deny-default w/ 6 allow rules, fail2ban 2,573 total bans / 29 active, public ports 22/80/443/1212/1213/7575
- Tailscale docs: tailscale.com/kb (MagicDNS, ACLs, SSH, exit nodes, subnet routers, control-vs-data-plane)
- SS14 docs: docs.spacestation14.com (port-forwarding, hub registration)
- Ollama remote-access patterns: launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST, Tailscale-IP binding

## 2. Current State / Baseline

### 2.1 Network topology (today)

```
Internet
   ↓
zig-computer (VPS, 51.81.33.136)  ← all services live here
   • SSH :22 (LIMIT, fail2ban-protected) ← all 3 personal devices SSH here directly
   • nginx :80/:443 (linearb.zig.computer, ss14.zig.computer)
   • reef-router :7575 (granola webhook)
   • SS14 game server :1212/:1213 UDP+TCP
   • ss14-watchdog.service
   • vs14-web.service (127.0.0.1:3300, fronted by nginx)
   • postgresql@17-main.service (127.0.0.1:5432 + 172.17.0.1:5432 for docker)
   • Docker: 5 vs14 obs containers (grafana, loki, prometheus, ss14-admin, mapserver)
   • ollama.service (127.0.0.1:11434)
   • phoenix :6006 + :4317 (LLM observability)
```

pico exists but is not yet on any shared network. metis + zig + iPhone independently SSH to zig-computer over the public internet via port 22.

### 2.2 UFW state (verified 2026-05-23)

```
22/tcp           LIMIT  Anywhere       (OpenSSH)
80,443/tcp       ALLOW  Anywhere       (Nginx)
1212             ALLOW  Anywhere       (SS14 live)
1213             ALLOW  Anywhere       (SS14 dev)
7575/tcp         ALLOW  Anywhere       (reef-router/granola)
44880            ALLOW  172.18.0.0/16  (prometheus, docker-bridge scoped)
5432/tcp         ALLOW  172.17.0.0/16  (postgres, docker-bridge scoped)
```

Default deny incoming, allow outgoing, deny routed.

### 2.3 Dotfiles baseline

- `mac.setup.sh` line 5: `brew install tmux jq gh ranger ... dicklesworthstone/tap/bv` — NO `ollama`, NO `tailscale`
- `ubuntu.setup.sh`: no Tailscale install
- No `tailscale/` dir
- No zig-zone runbook
- No Ollama plist (correct — Homebrew + `brew services` handles it)

### 2.4 Why the change

The user wants:
- Light, durable, non-locked-in mesh networking (Tailscale w/ Headscale exit option preserved)
- Workshop/server discipline (coding box ≠ service box)
- Local LLM inference at zero marginal cost via pico's Apple Silicon GPU
- Reach pico from anywhere (laptops in cafés, phone) via mesh, not by port-forwarding the home router
- Eventually run longer-interval agentic loops against pico (free model, no quota pressure)

## 3. Changes and Decisions

| Decision | Why | How |
|---|---|---|
| **Adopt Tailscale free tier** (not Headscale day-one) | Easy onboarding, peer-to-peer data plane preserves privacy, Headscale is the exit ramp if ever needed (drop-in compatible). 5 devices fits the 100-device limit comfortably. | `curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh \| sh` (Ubuntu); `brew install tailscale` (macOS); App Store (iOS). `sudo tailscale up` triggers browser SSO. |
| **MagicDNS on** | `<host>.<tailnet>.ts.net` names work transparently across the mesh. SSH/nginx configs become trivial. | Default-on; tailnet DNS name picked in admin console. |
| **Tailscale SSH = primary path; sshd port 22 = break-glass** | Tailscale-only is the #1 day-one mistake on remote VPS (coordination-server lockout risk). Current sshd posture (LIMIT + fail2ban: 2,573 historic bans, 29 currently banned) is doing real work. | Enable `tailscale up --ssh` on zig-computer + pico. ACL grants SSH to `tag:user-devices`. Keep `ufw limit 22/tcp` as is. |
| **No subnet router** | User has no other home-LAN devices that need tailnet exposure right now. One-line change to add later if a NAS/Pi appears. | Skip `--advertise-routes` flag on pico. |
| **No exit node** | Not needed — VPS already has public IP; user isn't trying to hide origin or geo-shift. | Skip `--advertise-exit-node` on any device. |
| **Migrate all hostable services zig-computer → pico** | Workshop/server discipline. M1 Max is a better server for everything except the public-edge role. | Phased (§ 4.4). |
| **Keep on zig-computer: nginx + reef-router + sshd-break-glass** | nginx is the public TLS terminator and DNS landing zone; reef-router needs public surface for granola webhook (external POST source); sshd stays as Tailscale-down recovery. | Don't touch these in migration; only re-point nginx upstreams to pico tailnet IPs. |
| **Ollama on pico binds to tailnet IP only** | Default `0.0.0.0` would expose the API to any home-LAN guest; `127.0.0.1` would block tailnet access. Tailnet-IP-only is the precise scope. | `launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST "$(tailscale ip -4):11434"` before `brew services start ollama`. |
| **SS14 migration is conditional** | UDP forwarding via nginx stream{} adds 10-20ms + jitter; SS14 may embed bind address in handshakes (untested per OQ-01). | Phase 4 begins with a feasibility test; spec defines abort criteria if test fails. |
| **Dotfiles additions stay light** | User's explicit preference. | New: `tailscale/acl.json`, `tailscale/README.md`, `agents/skills/zig-zone/SKILL.md`. Modified: `mac.setup.sh` (4 lines added), `ubuntu.setup.sh` (1 line added). |

## 4. Formal Specification

### 4.1 Device naming (tailnet hostnames = OS hostnames)

| Device | Tailnet name | MagicDNS | OS | Role |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OVH VPS | `zig-computer` | `zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net` | Ubuntu 25.10 | workshop + edge |
| Mac Studio M1 Max 64GB | `pico` | `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` | macOS | server farm |
| Personal MacBook | `metis` | `metis.<tailnet>.ts.net` | macOS | client |
| Work MacBook | `zig` | `zig.<tailnet>.ts.net` | macOS | client (subject to OQ-03) |
| iPhone | (iOS device name, lowercased) | `<phone>.<tailnet>.ts.net` | iOS | client via Termius |

Tailnet DNS name itself (the `<tailnet>` part) is decided in the Tailscale admin console — see OQ-04.

### 4.2 Tailscale ACL (`tailscale/acl.json`)

```jsonc
{
  // Single-user tailnet — full mesh, with tag-based permissions for future flexibility.
  "tagOwners": {
    "tag:server":  ["autogroup:admin"],  // zig-computer, pico
    "tag:client":  ["autogroup:admin"]   // metis, zig, iphone
  },

  "acls": [
    // All my devices can reach each other on any port. (Personal tailnet.)
    { "action": "accept", "src": ["autogroup:member"], "dst": ["*:*"] }
  ],

  "ssh": [
    // Tailscale SSH: clients can `tailscale ssh ubuntu@<server>` to the Linux box,
    // and `tailscale ssh <user>@<server>` to the Mac.
    {
      "action": "check",       // re-auth required every 12h for SSH session start
      "src":    ["tag:client"],
      "dst":    ["tag:server"],
      "users":  ["ubuntu", "autogroup:nonroot"]
    }
  ],

  "nodeAttrs": [
    // No exit nodes advertised in this spec. Re-evaluate post-Phase-5.
  ]
}
```

Tags assigned per-device in the Tailscale admin console (or via `tailscale up --advertise-tags=tag:server` on the device).

### 4.3 Dotfiles changes

**New files:**

```
dotfiles/tailscale/
├── acl.json         # source of truth for the Tailscale admin policy
└── README.md        # topology + recovery procedures (mirrors zig-zone skill)

dotfiles/agents/skills/zig-zone/
└── SKILL.md         # runbook — auto-loaded as /zig-zone
```

**Modified files:**

`dotfiles/mac.setup.sh` — line 5 gains `ollama tailscale`; new lines after:
```bash
# zig-zone: tailscale + ollama bring-up
sudo pmset -a sleep 0 displaysleep 0 disksleep 0 powernap 0
sudo tailscale up --ssh --advertise-tags=tag:server   # pico only; client devices: tag:client
launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST "$(tailscale ip -4):11434"
brew services start ollama
```

`dotfiles/ubuntu.setup.sh` — add:
```bash
# zig-zone: tailscale
curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh
sudo tailscale up --ssh --advertise-tags=tag:server
```

**No new `launchd/` dir.** `brew services start ollama` manages its own LaunchAgent; `OLLAMA_HOST` is provided to that agent via `launchctl setenv`.

### 4.4 Migration phases

Each phase has explicit acceptance criteria (§ 5 test cases). A phase isn't done until its tests pass on the live system.

#### Phase 0 — Mesh bootstrap (no service changes)

1. Pre-generate one Tailscale auth key per device in the admin console (or use browser SSO)
2. Install Tailscale on all 5 devices (`zig-computer`, `pico`, `metis`, `zig`, iPhone)
3. `sudo tailscale up` on each (no SSH yet, no tags yet — just join the tailnet)
4. Verify `tailscale status` on each device lists all 5 peers
5. Verify MagicDNS: `ping pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` from `zig-computer` resolves
6. Verify connection type: `tailscale status` shows `direct` between `zig-computer` and `pico` (NOT `relay` — flag for investigation if so)

**Reversible:** `sudo tailscale down` on each, uninstall. No service changes yet.

#### Phase 1 — Hardening

1. Commit `tailscale/acl.json` + `tailscale/README.md` to dotfiles
2. Paste ACL into Tailscale admin console; verify policy compiles
3. Re-run `sudo tailscale up --ssh --advertise-tags=tag:server` on `zig-computer` + `pico`
4. Re-run `sudo tailscale up --ssh --advertise-tags=tag:client` on `metis`, `zig`, iPhone
5. Test: `tailscale ssh ubuntu@zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net` from `metis` succeeds
6. Test: `tailscale ssh user@pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` from `metis` succeeds
7. Test: existing `ssh ubuntu@51.81.33.136` on port 22 still works (break-glass intact)
8. Optionally tighten UFW: `sudo ufw allow from <home-cidr> to any port 22 proto tcp` + delete the bare LIMIT rule. **Conditional on OQ-02.** If home IP is dynamic, skip this — keep the rate-limit alone.

#### Phase 2 — Ollama migration

1. On `pico`: `brew install ollama` (if not yet), `launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST "$(tailscale ip -4):11434"`, `brew services start ollama`
2. `ollama pull llama3.2:3b` (smoke-test model) + `ollama pull qwen2.5:7b` (real model)
3. From `zig-computer`: `curl http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:11434/api/tags` returns 200 with model list
4. On `zig-computer`: `sudo systemctl stop ollama && sudo systemctl disable ollama`
5. Verify on `zig-computer`: `curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags` returns connection-refused
6. Update any client config (Claude Code, agentic loops) to point at `http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:11434` instead of `localhost:11434`

#### Phase 3 — vacation-station-14 + postgres + observability containers

1. On `pico`: install postgresql 17 via `brew install postgresql@17`, restore from a `pg_dump` of `vacation_station` + `vacation_station_mapserver`
2. On `pico`: install Docker (Docker Desktop or Colima), restore the 5 obs containers from their compose files
3. On `pico`: run `vs14-web` (Next.js) bound to `127.0.0.1:3300` (or the tailnet IP — see OQ-05)
4. On `zig-computer`: update nginx vhost for `linearb.zig.computer` (the vs14-web upstream) to `proxy_pass http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:3300`
5. `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx`
6. Verify: `https://linearb.zig.computer/<vs14-route>` resolves and renders
7. On `zig-computer`: `sudo systemctl stop vs14-web postgresql@17-main && sudo systemctl disable vs14-web postgresql@17-main`
8. Stop + remove the 5 obs containers from `zig-computer`'s docker

#### Phase 4 — SS14 game server (conditional on feasibility test)

**Test first.** Before any migration:

1. On `pico`: install SS14 server + watchdog into `/opt/ss14-watchdog/` per upstream docs
2. Configure SS14 to bind `0.0.0.0:1212` (or the tailnet IP — TBD by test)
3. On `zig-computer`: write nginx stream{} config:
   ```nginx
   stream {
       upstream ss14_udp { server pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:1212; }
       server { listen 1212 udp reuseport; proxy_pass ss14_udp; proxy_timeout 30s; }
       upstream ss14_tcp { server pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:1212; }
       server { listen 1212; proxy_pass ss14_tcp; }
   }
   ```
4. `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx`
5. **Test:** stop the SS14 game server on `zig-computer`; have a real client connect to `ss14.zig.computer:1212` (or the public IP); play for 5+ minutes; verify no disconnect, no unexpected reconnect attempts to a `*.ts.net` address (would indicate SS14 leaked its bind address — see OQ-01)
6. If test passes: proceed with migration — disable `ss14-watchdog.service` on `zig-computer`, leave the nginx stream{} forwarding live
7. **If test fails:** abort migration. SS14 stays on `zig-computer`. Update spec with findings. The other migrations (Ollama, vs14-web, etc.) are independent and still complete.

#### Phase 5 — Done state verification

Final state asserted by tests TC14 + TC15 (§ 5). On `zig-computer`, `systemctl list-units --state=running --type=service` shows only: edge services (nginx, sshd, fail2ban), system services (systemd-*, dbus, cron, ufw, unattended-upgrades, qemu-guest-agent, etc.), reef-router (`*:7575`), tailscaled, and the user dev environment. No `ss14-*`, no `vs14-*`, no `postgresql`, no `ollama`, no docker workload containers.

### 4.5 Failure conditions + recovery

| Failure | Symptom | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| Tailscale coordination server down | New SSH sessions via `tailscale ssh` fail to authenticate | Fall back to `ssh ubuntu@51.81.33.136` (LIMITed sshd is intact); existing sessions stay live |
| pico tailscaled crashes / Mac reboots | nginx upstream `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` unreachable; vs14-web + SS14 + Ollama all 502/timeout | `brew services restart tailscale` on pico (or full reboot — LaunchDaemon brings it back); nginx upstream auto-recovers on next request |
| Home ISP outage / CGNAT relay fallback | Latency to pico spikes from 5ms → 30ms+; SS14 players notice; Ollama still works but slower | No fast fix; ride out outage. If chronic, investigate Tailscale DERP relay region selection or ISP change |
| OVH VPS rebooted / public IP changes | DNS still points at old IP; all public traffic dead | OVH KVM console; check `ip addr`; update DNS A records if IP changed (rare) |
| Locked out entirely (Tailscale + sshd both unreachable) | Can't reach `zig-computer` at all | OVH KVM web console (documented in zig-zone skill); break-glass user/password reset path |
| Auth key compromise | Unknown device joins tailnet | Tailscale admin console: revoke key, audit log shows affected devices, force re-auth |

## 5. Test Cases (≥10)

```
TEST: TC01-mesh-status
INPUT:    `tailscale status` on each of 5 devices
EXPECTED: each output lists the other 4 devices with hostname, IP (100.x.y.z), connection type
RATIONALE: confirms mesh bootstrap succeeded for all peers

TEST: TC02-magicdns-resolve
INPUT:    `dig +short pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` from zig-computer
EXPECTED: returns pico's 100.x.y.z tailnet IP
RATIONALE: MagicDNS is the lookup substrate every later config depends on

TEST: TC03-direct-connection
INPUT:    `tailscale status` on zig-computer, grep for pico
EXPECTED: line shows `direct` (not `relay`)
RATIONALE: relay fallback would multiply latency 4-10x — flag for ISP/NAT investigation

TEST: TC04-tailscale-ssh-from-laptop
INPUT:    `tailscale ssh ubuntu@zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net` from metis
EXPECTED: shell prompt, no password/key prompt, identity proven via Tailscale
RATIONALE: validates Tailscale SSH is the primary path; ACL policy is correct

TEST: TC05-break-glass-ssh-still-works
INPUT:    `ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@51.81.33.136` from metis (with the existing ~/.ssh/id_*) using port 22
EXPECTED: succeeds (LIMITed sshd still accepts)
RATIONALE: confirms the break-glass survives Tailscale enablement

TEST: TC06-fail2ban-still-active
INPUT:    `sudo fail2ban-client status sshd` on zig-computer
EXPECTED: jail status active, banned count nonzero, journal matches updated within last 24h
RATIONALE: existing brute-force protection is the safety net for break-glass exposure

TEST: TC07-ollama-on-pico-tailnet-only
INPUT:    `lsof -iTCP:11434 -sTCP:LISTEN` on pico
EXPECTED: shows only one bind on the tailnet IP (100.x.y.z), NOT 0.0.0.0 and NOT 127.0.0.1
RATIONALE: misbound Ollama either leaks to home LAN guests (0.0.0.0) or blocks tailnet clients (127.0.0.1) — both wrong

TEST: TC08-ollama-reachable-from-vps
INPUT:    `curl -s http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:11434/api/tags` from zig-computer
EXPECTED: HTTP 200, JSON body with `models` array containing the pulled models
RATIONALE: validates the canonical inference path end-to-end

TEST: TC09-old-ollama-gone
INPUT:    `curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags` on zig-computer; `systemctl is-enabled ollama.service`
EXPECTED: connection-refused; `disabled`
RATIONALE: no two Ollama processes; agentic clients can't accidentally hit the old endpoint

TEST: TC10-vs14-web-via-tailnet
INPUT:    `curl -sI https://linearb.zig.computer/<known-vs14-route>` from anywhere on internet
EXPECTED: HTTP 200, Server: nginx, content originates from pico
RATIONALE: confirms nginx-on-zig-computer → tailnet → vs14-web-on-pico works for public HTTPS traffic

TEST: TC11-postgres-not-on-tailnet
INPUT:    `nc -zv pico.<tailnet>.ts.net 5432` from zig-computer
EXPECTED: connection refused (postgres on pico binds 127.0.0.1 only)
RATIONALE: postgres has no need to be tailnet-reachable — vs14-web on pico hits it via loopback

TEST: TC12-ss14-feasibility (Phase 4 gate)
INPUT:    real client connects to ss14.zig.computer:1212; play session for 5 minutes; tcpdump on the client for any packets to *.ts.net hostnames
EXPECTED: gameplay smooth, no client→tailnet-hostname packets observed
RATIONALE: validates SS14/Robust Toolbox doesn't leak its bind address — the load-bearing OQ-01 question

TEST: TC13-iphone-ssh-via-termius
INPUT:    on iPhone, open Termius, connect to `zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net:22` (or use Tailscale SSH if Termius supports the protocol)
EXPECTED: shell connects
RATIONALE: confirms iOS device is fully usable on tailnet; no port-forwarding hacks needed

TEST: TC14-zig-computer-end-state
INPUT:    `systemctl list-units --state=running --type=service --no-pager` on zig-computer (post-Phase-5)
EXPECTED: list contains NO entries matching `ss14-*`, `vs14-*`, `postgresql@*`, `ollama`, or vs14 docker containers; DOES contain nginx, sshd, fail2ban, reef-router, tailscaled
RATIONALE: validates workshop/server split — the entire migration goal

TEST: TC15-acl-policy-deny-unrelated
INPUT:    paste ACL into Tailscale admin "policy editor" preview; check src=tag:client, dst=non-tagged-device:22
EXPECTED: policy denies
RATIONALE: confirms ACL doesn't accidentally grant promiscuous access; future devices added without tags are isolated by default

TEST: TC16-headscale-key-portability (smoke)
INPUT:    `tailscale netcheck`; review private key location on a Mac (~/Library/Application Support/Tailscale/) and Linux (/var/lib/tailscale/)
EXPECTED: keys are local files; documented in zig-zone skill as part of recovery
RATIONALE: confirms not-locked-in property; if Tailscale Inc. ever becomes hostile, keys can move to Headscale
```

## 6. Open Questions

- **OQ-01 (P1): Does SS14 / Robust Toolbox engine embed its bind address in network handshakes or hub-advertise calls?**
  - Recommendation: don't speculate — answer empirically in Phase 4 step 5 (the feasibility test). If test fails, SS14 stays on zig-computer. The other phases proceed independently.
  - Affects: Phase 4 (decisive)

- **OQ-02 (P1): Is your home/office public IP stable enough to allowlist on port 22?**
  - Recommendation: USER DECIDES. If stable + known: `ufw allow from <home-cidr> to any port 22 proto tcp` and delete the bare LIMIT rule — collapses brute-force surface to zero. If dynamic: keep `ufw limit 22/tcp` alone (current state; works well per fail2ban data: 2,573 bans logged).
  - Affects: Phase 1 step 8

- **OQ-03 (P2): Does the work laptop (`zig`) allow installing Tailscale per your employer's device-management policy?**
  - Recommendation: 30-second check before Phase 0. If blocked: `zig` is excluded from the tailnet; SSH from `zig` to `zig-computer` continues on public port 22 (LIMITed), which is fine — it just doesn't get Tailscale SSH.
  - Affects: device count in Phase 0, ACL membership

- **OQ-04 (P2): What tailnet DNS name should we pick?**
  - Tailscale admin offers a list of pickable names (cat-themed pairs like `tail-XXXX.ts.net`, or memorable ones). Examples: `zig-zone.ts.net` if available, else something like `dapper-narwhal.ts.net`.
  - Recommendation: pick a memorable one — every URL in the spec references it. Decide before Phase 0 step 4 (the first `tailscale status` test, where the name appears).
  - Affects: cosmetics only — no functional impact

- **OQ-05 (P2): Should vs14-web on pico bind to `127.0.0.1:3300` or to pico's tailnet IP?**
  - Option A: `127.0.0.1:3300` + `pico` runs its own local nginx that proxies tailnet-IP-port-443 → 127.0.0.1:3300 → vs14-web — clean separation, vs14 isolated from tailnet
  - Option B: vs14-web binds directly to pico's tailnet IP:3300; nginx-on-zig-computer proxies to it directly — one fewer hop, no local nginx on pico
  - Recommendation: Option B (simpler, fewer moving parts). vs14-web is not auth'd anyway; tailnet is the trust boundary.
  - Affects: Phase 3 steps 3 + 4

- **OQ-06 (P3): Phoenix observability (`*:4317` gRPC on zig-computer — currently UFW-protected) — does it migrate to pico in Phase 3, or stay on zig-computer?**
  - Phoenix is LLM observability tied to Ollama usage. If Ollama moves to pico, traces become tail-traffic to phoenix-on-zig-computer (cross-tailnet for every request). Cleaner to move phoenix to pico in Phase 3.
  - Recommendation: move with Ollama (Phase 2 or 3 — depends on dependency chain).
  - Affects: Phase 2/3 scope; can also defer to a follow-up spec

- **OQ-07 (P3): Should we set up Tailscale's HTTPS cert for `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` (`tailscale cert`) so internal traffic VPS→pico is TLS-encrypted on top of WireGuard?**
  - Recommendation: NO. WireGuard already encrypts the underlying link. Adding TLS is belt-and-suspenders w/ no real threat-model benefit. Reconsider only if a future application demands HTTPS upstream.
  - Affects: nothing day-one

## 7. Future Considerations

- **Fine-tuning workflow** — separate spec. Add MLX-LM + a Python venv on pico, LaunchDaemon for `mlx_lm.server` if we want a second always-on inference endpoint, dotfiles entry for the venv requirements. Estimated complexity: small spec, ~3 phases.
- **Headscale migration** — if Tailscale Inc. ever changes pricing/policy. The dotfiles work is mostly the same; only the coordination server URL changes. Devices keep their WireGuard keys; you redirect them at `headscale.zig.computer` (a service on zig-computer's edge nginx).
- **Subnet routing** — if a NAS/Pi/Home Assistant box appears on the home LAN, advertise `192.168.x.0/24` via `tailscale up --advertise-routes`. One-line change.
- **Exit node** — if you want to route phone/laptop traffic through home for geo or trust reasons. `tailscale up --advertise-exit-node` on pico (or zig-computer).
- **Additional VPS** — if you want geographic redundancy on the edge, a second VPS with its own public IP joining the tailnet as `corsair` (different cloud, different country). nginx vhosts mirror.
- **Tailscale Funnel** — if you want to expose a single pico service publicly without going through zig-computer (rare — you have zig-computer for that). Per-service decision.
- **Per-service ACL refinement** — current ACL is full mesh among autogroup:member. If you ever add a household member or a contractor to the tailnet, the ACL becomes more granular per `tag:server`/`tag:client` and per-port.
- **Audit logging** — Tailscale paid tier offers audit logs. Free tier doesn't. If audit becomes important, that's the upgrade trigger.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] Section 1.4 sources documented (audit skill, 3 research briefs, live audit run, Tailscale/SS14/Ollama docs cited)
 - [x] Sections 2 and 3 cite existing code/services by reference (UFW state verified, mac.setup.sh line 5 cited, current systemd inventory matches audit skill Phase 6)
 - [x] Section 5 has 16 test cases (TC01-TC16) each with INPUT/EXPECTED/RATIONALE
 - [x] Section 6 lists 7 open questions, each with priority + recommendation
 - [x] Each migration phase has explicit acceptance criteria mapped to TC# in section 5
 - [x] Failure conditions + recovery documented (section 4.5)

title: hooks: pre-commit-checks Bead-trailer carve-out misses :outbox_tray: (distribute)
description:
## Problem
pre-commit-checks.sh's Bead-trailer block (shipped in dotfiles-5q1)
exempts meta-commits by gitmoji — :card_file_box: / :broom: / :dollar:
— but misses :outbox_tray:, the gitmoji /distribute uses for
harness-sync commits. A `:outbox_tray: distribute: sync from dotfiles`
commit has no owning bead and is wrongly blocked.

## Symptom
Caught immediately: the /distribute sync commit to ~/linearb/skills
this session is blocked by the hook just shipped in dotfiles-5q1.

## Cause
The carve-out gitmoji alternation was incomplete — :outbox_tray: was
overlooked when enumerating meta-commit classes.

## Fix
Add :outbox_tray: to the carve-out alternation; cover it with a test
case. (This is the "carve-out must be maintained" cost the hooks
analysis flagged for the Bead-trailer block.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] :outbox_tray: distribute commits exempt from the Bead-trailer block
- [ ] test-pre-commit-checks.sh covers it and stays green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] :outbox_tray: added to the carve-out; distribute commits pass
 - [ ] test-pre-commit-checks.sh covers it and is green

title: blightmud: narrow ifmud gag to keepalive-only responses
description:
## Problem
The ifmud keepalive's gag mechanism used a time window (suppress_until) to bypass gagging during manual idle! invocations. Side effect: any user-typed who / idle / w also got silently gagged for 90 seconds after the keepalive, because the trigger matched the User-On-Idle header indiscriminately.

## Fix
Replace the time-based suppress with a boolean gag_next flag set by the keepalive itself. The trigger only gags when the keepalive flagged it; everything else (user-typed socializing commands) flows through naturally. Reset the flag on the End-of-List footer.

Also drop the manual idle! alias — no longer needed; user types idle directly and sees the response.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Keepalive response gagged (no spam every 90s)
- [x] User-typed who/idle/w fully visible
- [x] State backfill handles /load over older state tables

title: hooks: wire the .offboard-pending marker (documented but never created)
description:
## Problem
/offboard and /onboard both document a `.offboard-pending` marker:
/offboard claims pre-compact.sh + session-end.sh create it, and
/onboard Step 0 checks for it to run /offboard retroactively. No hook
actually creates the file — session-end.sh only flushes bead state.

## Symptom
The retroactive-offboard safety net is dead code. A session that ends
without /offboard is never caught by the next session's /onboard,
because the marker /onboard checks for is never written.

## Cause
session-end.sh was never given marker-creation logic; the skill docs
describe a mechanism that was never implemented.

## Source
Surfaced 2026-05-21 by the harness hooks-analysis review.

## Fix
Wire session-end.sh to drop .offboard-pending at the repo root unless
HEAD committed this session's handoff note (= /offboard ran). Keep
pre-compact.sh out of it — a same-session post-compaction /onboard
would misread its own marker. Correct the /offboard + /onboard docs;
gitignore the marker.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] session-end.sh creates .offboard-pending when /offboard didn't run
- [ ] no marker when /offboard ran (HEAD committed the handoff note)
- [ ] skipped inside worktrees
- [ ] /offboard + /onboard docs corrected; .offboard-pending gitignored
- [ ] regression test test-session-end.sh passes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] session-end.sh wires the marker correctly (offboard-ran detection + worktree skip)
 - [ ] /offboard + /onboard docs corrected; marker gitignored
 - [ ] test-session-end.sh passes
